name: Tiger Memory MCP Server
what_it_does: Official MCP server from Timescale for Tiger — a memory and context management system built on PostgreSQL and pgvector. Enables AI agents to persist, retrieve, and manage long-term memory through semantic vector search — providing durable agent memory backed by Timescale's PostgreSQL infrastructure.
use_cases: Persistent agent memory across conversations using semantic vector search
Storing and retrieving structured knowledge from long-running AI agent workflows
Building agent memory systems on PostgreSQL/pgvector infrastructure
Managing AI context windows with selective memory retrieval from Timescale
not_for: Simple session-only memory without persistence needs
Non-AI agent use cases without semantic search requirements
best_when: A team building production AI agents needs durable memory with semantic retrieval — backed by Timescale's PostgreSQL infrastructure with pgvector for vector similarity search.
avoid_when: You use other vector databases (Pinecone, Weaviate, Qdrant) for memory. Timescale Tiger is PostgreSQL-centric — evaluate if your infrastructure aligns.

auth.notes: Timescale service credentials required. PostgreSQL connection string with pgvector. Timescale Cloud or self-hosted PostgreSQL.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Timescale Cloud free trial; limited compute and storage
pricing.paid_tiers: Timescale Cloud pricing by compute and storage
pricing.notes: Timescale Cloud has a free trial. Self-hosted PostgreSQL with pgvector is fully free. MCP server is free open source from official Timescale org.

performance.rate_limits: Bounded by PostgreSQL connection limits and query complexity

known_agent_gotchas: pgvector embedding dimensions must match your embedding model — configure before storing memories
Memory retrieval quality depends on embedding model quality — use consistent models
Connection pooling important for agents making frequent memory queries
Official from Timescale — high quality but Tiger is relatively new compared to other Timescale products
agent_readiness.security_notes: Official Timescale MCP. HTTPS to Timescale Cloud or local PostgreSQL. Database credentials required. Vector memory may contain sensitive agent context. SOC2 compliant.

security_score_components.security_notes: Official Timescale. HTTPS. Database credentials. pgvector memory may contain sensitive agent data. SOC2.
